summary: Frontend-only e2e testing completed for the Rolplay redesign (7 pages, animations, CTAs, forms, accordions, copy-to-clipboard, mobile menu). All 7 routes load. All critical interactive elements work. Toasts fire correctly via sonner. Brand 'Rol' (red) + 'Play' (white) logo correctly rendered. External CTAs have target=_blank + rel='noopener noreferrer'. WhatsApp orb links to wa.me/15797986707. Mobile hamburger menu opens correctly at 390px viewport. EN/ES toggle works. Glassmorphism nav appears on scroll. Hero counters animate (91+ corporate clients, 8,750+ active users). Award badge visible. Falling particles + neural-network canvas render. Benefits 3 accordions and FAQs 8 accordions all clickable. Success Stories rank numbers '4th' and '2nd' render. Contact page email & phone copy buttons trigger 'Email copied' toast. Both compact (Home) and full (Contact) ContactForm submissions show 'Message sent · We'll be in touch shortly' sonner toast. About page globe SVG renders. Achievements shows two award cards. No JS pageerrors thrown. Only 1 minor React console warning detected.
action_items: Minor: Fix React console warning 'Unknown event handler property onViewportEnter. It will be ignored.' on Home.jsx line ~366. The outer <section data-testid="testimonial-section" onViewportEnter={...}> is a plain DOM element — onViewportEnter only works on framer-motion components. Either remove the duplicate onViewportEnter on the <section> (the inner motion.div already triggers setQuoteStart) or convert the outer section to motion.section. Functionality (typewriter starts) works because the inner motion.div fires it; this is a console-noise/code-quality issue only.
critical_code_review_comments: Home.jsx (line 366) uses onViewportEnter on a plain <section>; framer-motion only honors that prop on motion.* components. Resulting React DOM warning. Fix: drop the prop on <section> (motion.div inside already covers it) or use motion.section.

ScrollProgress component is intentionally width:0 at scroll top (scaleX from useScroll) — testid is in DOM, but is_visible() returns false until user scrolls. Acceptable behavior, just note for testers.
Files are well-modularized; all interactive elements expose data-testid attributes. No 700+ line files spotted in pages/components.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth on this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 7 routes load and all interactions work end-to-end. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://sales-ai-labs.preview.emergentagent.com. Sonner toasts render in top-right; query selector '[data-sonner-toast]' returns toast text. Wait ~1500ms after submit (700ms fake delay + render). Clipboard tests need context.grant_permissions(['clipboard-read','clipboard-write']). No backend exists — contact forms are MOCKED client-side only.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Single console warning RCA: framer-motion's onViewportEnter is a prop on motion components only; passing it to a native <section> causes React to emit 'Unknown event handler property' warning. Reproduction: load /, observe console. Mitigation: remove duplicate onViewportEnter from outer <section> OR change <section> to <motion.section>.
